X-H2S COMPANION

Tutorial · Presets · SOS · Manual · Exercises

More

Phone setup: FUJIFILM XApp

Set it up once, benefit three times over — ideally before the trip:

  1. Pair it: install the “FUJIFILM XApp” (App Store / Play Store), turn on Bluetooth, tap “Pair camera” in the app and follow the instructions.
  2. Settings backup: use the XApp's save/restore camera settings feature (“Register/Restore Camera Settings”). It lets you restore every preset and menu with a single tap in an emergency — the safety net against accidentally changed presets (Auto Update!). Back up once you've verified your setup.
  3. Geo-tagging: allow location sync in the app and on the camera set MENU → Network/USB → GEOTAGGING → ON. Every photo then carries its shooting location automatically.
  4. Transfer images: select & send from playback — or turn on automatic transfer.

Bluetooth barely uses any battery; Wi-Fi only turns on during a transfer.

OFFICIALFujifilm's XApp info pageDownload links and a feature overview

Backing up on the road — three ways

Way 1 · Straight to an Android phone: turn the camera on and connect it to the phone with a USB-C cable — the camera shows up as a drive, just drag the images over in the Files app.

Way 2 · Manually via a USB-C hub: plug the SD card into the hub and copy the files to an SSD/thumb drive. Note: read the CFexpress card with the included combo reader (SD + CFE) — or just wait until you're home and do it on a computer. That's perfectly fine, since the photos are already backed up on the SD card too.

Way 3 · With the FUJIFILM XApp: pair the camera (see the card above), then select images and transfer them. To save space, turn on the resize feature: MENU → Network/USB → Image Transfer/Resize ON, or the size option in the XApp — sends smaller, phone-friendly files.

If a transfer ever hiccups: don't worry, just try again — disconnect, reopen the app, toggle Bluetooth off/on if needed. That fixes it almost every time.

Memory cards: backup to both cards

This camera is set to BACKUP across both card slots — slot 1 = CFexpress, slot 2 = SD. That means:

Every photo (HEIF and RAW) is written to both cards at once — an automatic backup copy. If one card fails or gets lost, no image is gone.

Videos, on the other hand, are always saved only to the CFexpress card (slot 1) — so the SD card never has videos on it.

Both cards fill up in parallel with photos; whichever is smaller or fuller becomes the limit. If the camera says “full”, swap or clear the affected card — then you're good to keep shooting.

View/change this setting: MENU → wrench icon → DATA STORAGE SETTING
